question_text: An electromagnet has 50 coils of wire wrapped around a core and uses a current of 2 A. If the current is increased to 4 A with all other factors staying the same, by what factor does the strength of the magnetic field increase?
correct_answer: 2 times (or the magnetic field strength doubles)
explanation: The strength of an electromagnet is directly proportional to the current flowing through it. If the current doubles from 2 A to 4 A, the magnetic field strength also doubles. This relationship is linear, so doubling the current gives twice the field strength.
